critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Verdict: critique_wins

Supervision mismatch (impression vs. positive-only), feature mismatch (audio+skip vs. play-only), and no intermediate baseline. The Recall@100 gap measures training-regime and feature advantages, not neural architecture quality.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Verdict: critique_wins

The supervision source mismatch alone is sufficient to invalidate the neural-retrieval-quality claim. A matrix factorization trained on impression data with negatives would have a substantial advantage over the legacy positive-only baseline on its own, without any neural components. The paper acknowledges this cost trade-off explicitly ('too expensive to reconstruct').

ASSESSOR 3:
Verdict: critique_wins

The feature set mismatch (audio embeddings, skip behavior) and supervision mismatch together compound to produce the 12-point gap. No ablation was run. The paper frames this as evidence of neural architecture quality when it is evidence of a better-equipped system overall.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous critique_wins. Strong convergence across all three assessors. The paper's own acknowledgment that impression-level negatives weren't provided to the baseline due to cost confirms the supervision mismatch is deliberate and material.
